mission: Study the best near-term follow-on products for ChapAI, package the most profitable and realistic opportunities, and keep one validated next-build option ready while the current products monetize.
goals: Maintain one realistic next-product recommendation tied to near-term revenue.
Prefer products that reuse the current tutor, content, and workflow stack.
Surface validation plans that cost little and can ship fast once CCRN/NCLEX are stable.
workflowContract: Keep one shortlist, one recommendation memo, and one validation plan current.
Promote only next-product ideas that are realistic for the current stack and capital level.
Escalate only the highest-leverage next product instead of creating a sprawling wishlist.
durableMemory: The next product should reuse the current question, tutor, persistence, and marketing stack where possible.
Products that improve working nurses directly may become stronger follow-ons once exam prep starts cash-flowing.
A short memo plus a cheap validation plan is more useful than a giant speculative roadmap.
The strongest next product is an ICU report and handoff assistant.
The next build should wait until the exam products mostly shift into question growth plus marketing mode.
High-intent SEO and comparison pages are now part of the near-term validation stack for the exam products.
activeContext: One validated adjacent healthcare SaaS memo
